message:
fix(storage,backup,diagnostic,telemetry): apply busy_timeout to every read Connection — class 4 SQLITE_BUSY hardening

Multi-pass bug-hunt sweep found ~75 production sites that called
Connection::open without setting busy_timeout, so any read query
contended with the live writer would surface SQLITE_BUSY immediately
instead of letting SQLite retry internally for the configured 5s window.

Per /deadlock-finder-and-fixer Class 4: every connection must set
busy_timeout. Existing code already did this in
record_policy_denial_audit_blocking, session_restore::open_conn,
session_retention, and snapshot_engine — these were the holdouts.

storage.rs
  - New `open_read_storage_conn` helper (above the existing
    record_policy_denial_audit_blocking helper) opens + sets 5s
    busy_timeout in one place.
  - Replaces 75+ inline `Connection::open(db_path.as_str()).map_err(...)`
    spawn_blocking sites and self.db_path.as_str() variants. Reads now
    consistently retry under writer contention.
  - database_stats / db_check / repair_database / migration_plan_for_path
    / migration_status_for_path: explicit busy_timeout on the conn — these
    return diagnostic reports that previously falsely flagged a healthy
    DB as broken under writer load.

backup.rs
  - backup_database / dump_database_sql / gather_stats / verify_backup
    all open the LIVE source DB; without busy_timeout the online backup
    aborts at the first read-lock attempt. Adds 5s timeout at each.

diagnostic.rs::gather_db_health
  - Same fix; diagnostic queries previously failed cosmetic checks under
    contention.

telemetry.rs::TelemetryStore::open
  - Long-lived persistent connection; matches the standard
    "PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL; synchronous=NORMAL; busy_timeout=5000;
    foreign_keys=ON" recipe.

storage/mmap_store.rs::SqliteFallbackStore::open
  - Same recipe applied; the scrollback-fallback connection is
    long-lived too.

Other fixes in this pass:

search/chunk_vector_store.rs::ensure_semantic_store_metadata
  - Wrap the read → embedding-DELETE → metadata-UPSERT sequence in a
    BEGIN IMMEDIATE / COMMIT transaction. Without it (ft-xphjm), a crash
    between DELETE and the metadata insert leaves embeddings gone but
    the version mismatch persists, triggering a second invalidation
    that races with new generations. Also adds 5s busy_timeout on the
    long-lived store connection.

recording.rs (record_segment + record_segment_with_cx)
  - Credit `bytes_raw` only after the underlying record_output write
    succeeds. The new u32 payload-size guard (c3b59e4a) can reject
    oversize payloads, but the previous order bumped bytes_raw before
    the call — inflating the stat with content that never landed.
